frameworks.name: EU AI Act
frameworks.description: EU Artificial Intelligence Act - risk-based governance framework for AI systems
frameworks.penalty: Up to €35M or 7% of global turnover

frameworks.name: GDPR
frameworks.description: General Data Protection Regulation - personal data protection for EU operations
frameworks.penalty: Up to €20M or 4% of global turnover

frameworks.name: GDPR/BDSG
frameworks.description: GDPR with German BDSG additions - works council, employee data, DPO thresholds
frameworks.urgency: Works council can block AI deployment

frameworks.name: ISO 42001
frameworks.description: ISO/IEC 42001 - AI Management System certification standard
frameworks.penalty: Loss of contracts and market access

frameworks.name: ISO 27001
frameworks.penalty: Loss of certification and procurement exclusion

frameworks.name: SOC 2
frameworks.description: SOC 2 Type II - security trust standard for SaaS and service organisations
frameworks.penalty: Failed sales cycles and lost contracts

frameworks.name: DORA
frameworks.description: Digital Operational Resilience Act - ICT risk for financial entities
frameworks.penalty: Up to 1% of average daily turnover per day

frameworks.name: NIS2
frameworks.description: NIS2 Directive - cybersecurity for essential and important entities
frameworks.urgency: NIS2UmsuCG in force December 2025 in Germany
frameworks.penalty: Up to €10M or 2% of global turnover. Personal management liability.

frameworks.name: EU MDR
frameworks.description: EU Medical Device Regulation - for AI-powered medical devices and SaMD
frameworks.urgency: Class IIa+ AI devices require Notified Body now
frameworks.penalty: Product withdrawal. Criminal liability for PRRC.

frameworks.name: DiGA
frameworks.description: German Digital Health Applications - BfArM listing for GKV reimbursement
frameworks.penalty: BfArM delisting. Loss of GKV reimbursement.

frameworks.name: KHZG
frameworks.description: Hospital Future Act - IT security and digitalisation for German hospitals
frameworks.urgency: DRG deductions up to 2% per case from 2026
frameworks.penalty: Funding clawback. Up to 2% DRG revenue deduction.

frameworks.name: HIPAA
frameworks.description: Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act - US health data privacy
frameworks.penalty: Up to $1.9M per violation category per year

frameworks.name: PCI DSS
frameworks.description: PCI DSS v4.0 - payment card data security standard
frameworks.penalty: Card brand fines $5K–$100K/month. Loss of card processing.

questions.question: Is ePHI encrypted both at rest and in transit, and is there a documented risk assessment justifying any unencrypted ePHI storage or transmission?
questions.rationale: Encryption is Addressable under current HIPAA but is proposed as Required under the 2025 NPRM. Unencrypted ePHI is a documented risk in 90%+ of investigated breaches.
questions.maturity_signal: ePHI is encrypted at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.2+). Encryption exceptions have documented risk assessments with compensating controls.

questions.question: Are audit controls implemented on all systems containing ePHI to record and examine access and activity, with audit logs reviewed regularly and retained for at least 6 years?

questions.maturity_signal: Audit logging is enabled on all ePHI systems. Logs are reviewed weekly via SIEM alerts. Retention period is ≥6 years. Anomalous access generates automated alerts.

questions.maturity_signal: Breach playbook documents 4-factor risk assessment, 60-day timeline, HHS web portal procedure, individual notification template, and media notification trigger (≥500 state residents).

questions.maturity_signal: Access controls implement minimum necessary by default. AI systems query only required PHI fields. Workforce requests for PHI beyond their role require supervisor approval.

questions.maturity_signal: PAN protection method is documented. PAN is absent from logs, test environments, and non-production systems. Annual PAN discovery scan confirms no unexpected PAN storage.
